query: I want to give a special CSS class to sway workspaces whose name starts with 'special:'. I wrote {workspace.name =~ "^special:.+$"} but I keep second-guessing the order — in bash I'd write [[ $name =~ pattern ]]. Which side is the regex in eww?
expected_behavior: Routes to the regex match =~ operator (SKILL.md '=~' section and OPERATORS.md 'Regex Match Operator')
States the wall explicitly: in eww the LEFT operand is the regex pattern and the RIGHT operand is the string being tested — pattern =~ string
Notes this is the OPPOSITE of bash ([[ string =~ pattern ]]) and Perl/Ruby

Confirms the user's example {workspace.name =~ "^special:.+$"} is actually backwards relative to the rule and the correct form puts the pattern on the left: {"^special:.+$" =~ workspace.name}, OR mentions matches(string, regex) as the string-first alternative
Mentions eww uses Rust regex syntax (no lookaheads)
notes: Guards against the reversed-operand wall. =~ in eww is regex-on-LEFT, string-on-RIGHT — backwards from bash/Perl/Ruby muscle memory. Getting it wrong silently tests the wrong direction and the class never applies. The skill also offers matches(string, regex) which uses the more familiar string-first order, so a good answer surfaces both.
